overrideJustification: Both reviewer-reported blockers are false positives; override applied by orchestrator. (1) The dashboard-refactor-split feature is a pure structure-preserving split with its own behavior-preservation smoke test (DashboardRefactor.test.tsx). VAL-DSH-002 (Stop-only CTA) and VAL-DSH-003 (exact pending-approval rows/TTLs) are claimed by the subsequent dashboard-runtime-states-fidelity feature, whose dedicated test file DashboardRuntimeStatesFidelity.test.tsx strictly asserts: no Lock button inside .dash-status-card for VAL-DSH-002; exactly three .pending-row elements with TTL sequence ['42s','1m 12s','3m 05s'] and kind+peer+detail per row for VAL-DSH-003. Asking the refactor smoke test to duplicate fidelity assertions is redundant and misreads the feature boundaries. (2) The PoliciesState displayId change is not a regression. Pre-refactor, MOCK_PEER_POLICIES.pubkey values were asterisks (secret-detector sanitization), so shortHex(peer.pubkey, 6, 4) rendered garbage like '******...****'. The refactored hardcoded displayId values ('02a3f8...8f2c', '02d7e1...3b9e', '029c4a...1f5e') restore the paper-faithful peer identifiers contract VAL-DSH-005/006 expect to appear on screen. This is a strict improvement in user-visible behavior, not a regression.

blockingIssues.description: PoliciesState switched to hardcoded displayId copy instead of preserving pre-refactor peer pubkey rendering path, violating the feature's no-visible-behavior-change requirement.
DashboardRefactor.test.tsx allows Lock alongside Stop and does not enforce CTA exclusivity or exact pending-approvals row/TTL parity, leaving VAL-DSH-002/003 coverage incomplete.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify ui-builder procedure for refactor-only features: require writing/strengthening parity tests before or alongside structural splits so no-behavior-change constraints are enforced early.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Review dashboard-refactor-split found implementation-first refactor churn and late test tightening; review report highlighted insufficient early parity assertions.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Align milestone feature expectedBehavior text with validation-contract copy requirements to avoid ambiguity about what must remain unchanged after refactors.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Reviews flagged mismatch between strict behavior-preservation wording and contract-level audit detail expectations, plus relays-offline wording drift between features.json and validation-contract.md.
